deck.name: Culinary & Dining Out
deck.description: Master the vocabulary of gastronomy, from cooking techniques to ordering at fine restaurants.

cards.definition: to cook the surface of food quickly at a high temperature
cards.context: You should sear the steak on both sides to lock in the juices.

cards.definition: to cook liquid just below the boiling point
cards.context: Let the sauce simmer for 20 minutes to develop the flavor.

cards.definition: cooked so as to be still firm when bitten (usually pasta)
cards.context: The spaghetti was perfectly al dente.

cards.definition: a small amount of food used to decorate a dish
cards.context: The chef added a sprig of parsley as a garnish.

cards.definition: meat cooked for a short time, remaining red and cool in the center
cards.context: I'd like my steak rare, please.

cards.definition: meat cooked to a pink center with a warm temperature
cards.context: Medium-rare is the most popular way to order a steak.

cards.definition: meat cooked thoroughly until no pink remains
cards.context: She prefers her burgers well-done for safety.

cards.definition: salty or spicy rather than sweet
cards.context: I prefer a savory breakfast like eggs over pancakes.

cards.definition: lacking flavor or spice; uninteresting to the taste
cards.context: This soup is a bit bland; it needs more salt.

cards.definition: having a bright, sharp, and fresh flavor (often citrus)
cards.context: The lemon tart has a wonderfully zesty finish.

cards.definition: to pour a thin stream of liquid over food
cards.context: Drizzle some olive oil over the salad before serving.

cards.definition: to fry quickly in a little hot fat
cards.context: Sauté the onions until they are translucent.

cards.definition: the flavor extracted from a substance by soaking it in liquid
cards.context: This oil has a subtle garlic infusion.

cards.definition: the primary dish of a multi-course meal
cards.context: For my entree, I'll have the roasted salmon.

cards.definition: a small dish served before the main meal
cards.context: Would you like to see the appetizer menu?

cards.definition: a smaller portion of food served alongside the main course
cards.context: Does the steak come with a side dish?

cards.definition: a substance such as a sauce or spice used to add flavor to food
cards.context: Ketchup and mustard are common condiments.

cards.definition: a person's appreciation of taste and flavor
cards.context: The wine tasting is designed to challenge your palate.

cards.definition: a wine expert who helps diners choose the right wine
cards.context: Ask the sommelier for a recommendation to match the fish.

cards.definition: referring to the year a wine was produced
cards.context: This 2015 vintage is exceptional.

cards.definition: a glass container used to aerate wine
cards.context: Pour the red wine into a decanter for an hour before serving.

cards.definition: special needs regarding what someone can or cannot eat
cards.context: Does anyone in your group have any dietary requirements?

cards.definition: food that does not contain the protein gluten
cards.context: We offer a wide variety of gluten-free options.

cards.definition: someone who does not eat any animal products
cards.context: Is this dessert suitable for vegans?

cards.definition: food that is grown or produced nearby
cards.context: We pride ourselves on using locally sourced ingredients.

cards.definition: made in a traditional or non-automated way
cards.context: This artisanal bread is baked fresh every morning.

cards.definition: to fry lightly and then stew slowly in a closed container
cards.context: Braised short ribs are a winter favorite.

cards.definition: sweets, chocolates, and other sugar-based products
cards.context: The shop is famous for its handmade confectionery.

cards.definition: a collection of small portions of several dishes served as a single meal
cards.context: We decided to go for the 7-course tasting menu.

cards.definition: a restaurant recognized for high quality by the Michelin Guide
cards.context: Dining at a Michelin-starred restaurant is a unique experience.

cards.definition: a small serving of food or drink intended to refresh the mouth between courses
cards.context: A lemon sorbet is a perfect palate cleanser.

cards.definition: referring to food that comes directly from a local farm to the restaurant
cards.context: This farm-to-table restaurant only serves what's in season.

cards.definition: cold cooked meats, often served with cheese and bread on a board
cards.context: We ordered a large charcuterie board to share.

cards.definition: to gradually decrease (e.g., wine finish)
cards.context: The wine has a long finish that slowly tapers off.

cards.definition: referring to wine or coffee that has a strong, rich flavor and texture
cards.context: This red wine is very full-bodied and pairs well with steak.

cards.definition: the characteristic smell of a wine or liqueur
cards.context: This Chardonnay has a complex bouquet of oak and vanilla.

cards.definition: a category of taste in food (besides sweet, sour, salt, and bitter), corresponding to the flavor of glutamates
cards.context: Mushrooms and soy sauce are rich in umami.

cards.definition: to cook sugar or the natural sugars in food until they turn brown and sweet
cards.context: Caramelize the onions slowly for the best flavor.

cards.definition: a sauce made by boiling a liquid until it thickens
cards.context: The balsamic reduction adds a sweet and tangy touch.

cards.definition: a fleshy boneless piece of meat or fish
cards.context: I'll have the salmon fillet, please.

cards.definition: the outer colored part of the peel of citrus fruit, used as flavoring
cards.context: Add some orange zest to the cake batter.

cards.definition: to cook by shimmering in a small amount of liquid
cards.context: Poached eggs are a classic breakfast choice.

cards.definition: to soak something in liquid to extract its flavor
cards.context: Infuse the cream with vanilla beans overnight.

cards.definition: a meal of several courses at a fixed price
cards.context: We opted for the three-course prix fixe menu.

cards.definition: a tiny, bite-sized appetizer provided free by the chef
cards.context: The dinner began with a delightful amuse-bouche of smoked salmon.
